seoTitle: Add Filters to Cards in Power BI With Visual-Level Filtering
metaDescription: Learn how to apply visual-level filtering to cards in Power BI Desktop so each card shows exactly the data you want, without affecting other visuals.
intro: Sometimes you need one card visual to show a specific slice of data without affecting the rest of your report. This guide covers how to apply visual-level filtering to cards in Power BI Desktop, keeping your key metrics precise and relevant.

steps.heading: Open Power BI Desktop
steps.body: Start with your report open in Power BI Desktop, with the card visual you want to filter already placed on the page.

steps.heading: Select your card visual
steps.body: Click on the specific card you want to apply a filter to, since visual-level filters only affect the selected visual rather than the whole page.

steps.heading: Apply filters
steps.body: Use the filter pane to add the specific criteria you want the card to reflect, narrowing its data independently from other visuals.

steps.heading: Review your filtered card
steps.body: Check the card after applying the filter to confirm it's displaying exactly the value or metric you intended.

steps.heading: Apply this to business analytics use cases
steps.body: Visual-level filtering on cards is useful for highlighting specific metrics, like sales for one region or one product line, without altering the rest of the report.

faq.question: Does a visual-level filter on a card affect other visuals on the page?
faq.answer: No, visual-level filters apply only to the specific visual they're set on, leaving other cards and charts on the page unaffected.

faq.question: Can I apply multiple filters to the same card?
faq.answer: Yes, you can stack multiple filter criteria on a single card to narrow it down to exactly the data you want to display.

faq.question: What's the difference between page-level and visual-level filtering?
faq.answer: Page-level filters affect every visual on the page, while visual-level filters apply only to the individual visual you've selected.

faq.question: Is visual-level filtering useful for beginners in Power BI?
faq.answer: Yes, it's a foundational skill that's approachable for beginners and useful immediately for building more precise, focused reports.
